title: Mouth bite animation on mouse hover

requirements.text: Add a mouth hover-triggered bite animation to the SVG smiley face in App.tsx. The current mouth is a static smile path (d="M 140 255 Q 200 315 260 255"). Replace it with an interactive mouth component that: (1) detects when the mouse enters the mouth region, (2) on mouse-enter, plays a quick 'open and bite' animation — the mouth opens wide (e.g., reveals an open ellipse or arc showing teeth/interior) for ~150–200ms, then snaps shut, (3) after biting, the mouth stays in a closed/flat or slightly-frowning state rather than the original smile, (4) when the mouse enters the mouth region again while it is closed/bitten, it repeats the open-bite-then-stay-closed sequence. The bite state resets only on the next mouse-enter. Use a React state variable (e.g., `mouthState: 'smile' | 'open' | 'bitten'`) and an SVG `<path>` or `<g>` to represent the three mouth states. The mouth's interactive hit area should use a transparent SVG `<ellipse>` or `<rect>` overlaid on the mouth region to reliably capture `onMouseEnter` events.

quotes: If the mouse comes over the mouth, it should open and bite, then stay closed until the mouse comes over again

acceptanceCriteria: When the mouse cursor moves over the mouth area, the mouth visibly opens (shows interior/teeth) for approximately 150–200ms.
After the open phase, the mouth animates to a closed/bitten position and remains there.
The closed/bitten mouth does not revert to the original smile on its own.
Moving the mouse over the closed mouth again triggers the full open-bite-closed sequence again.
The open and bite animation uses smooth SVG path transitions or rapid state changes that feel snappy and playful.
The mouse hit area for the mouth is large enough to be reliably triggered without pixel-perfect cursor placement.

constraints: Do not modify any files under frontend/src/hooks/ or frontend/src/main.tsx.
All mouth animation logic must be self-contained in frontend/src/App.tsx using React state and SVG.
The existing eye/blink/pupil behavior must remain unchanged.
nonGoals: No changes to the blinking or pupil-tracking logic.
No reset of the bitten state on mouse-leave — it stays bitten until next hover.
